speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Last night, I hear, they lay at Stony Stratford,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And at Northampton they do rest tonight.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Tomorrow or next day they will be here.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I long with all my heart to see the Prince.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I hope he is much grown since last I saw him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But I hear no; they say my son of York

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Has almost overta’en him in his growth.

speaker: YORK
text: Ay, mother, but I would not have it so.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why, my good cousin? It is good to grow.

speaker: YORK
text: Grandam, one night as we did sit at supper,

speaker: YORK
text: My uncle Rivers talked how I did grow

speaker: YORK
text: More than my brother. “Ay,” quoth my uncle Gloucester,

speaker: YORK
text: “Small herbs have grace; great weeds do grow apace.”

speaker: YORK
text: And since, methinks I would not grow so fast

speaker: YORK
text: Because sweet flowers are slow and weeds make haste.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Good faith, good faith, the saying did not hold

speaker: DUCHESS
text: In him that did object the same to thee!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: He was the wretched’st thing when he was young,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: So long a-growing and so leisurely,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That if his rule were true, he should be gracious.

speaker: YORK
text: And so no doubt he is, my gracious madam.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I hope he is, but yet let mothers doubt.

speaker: YORK
text: Now, by my troth, if I had been remembered,

speaker: YORK
text: I could have given my uncle’s Grace a flout

speaker: YORK
text: To touch his growth nearer than he touched mine.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: How, my young York? I prithee let me hear it.

speaker: YORK
text: Marry, they say my uncle grew so fast

speaker: YORK
text: That he could gnaw a crust at two hours old.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Twas full two years ere I could get a tooth.

speaker: YORK
text: Grandam, this would have been a biting jest.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I prithee, pretty York, who told thee this?

speaker: YORK
text: Grandam, his nurse.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: His nurse? Why, she was dead ere thou wast born.

speaker: YORK
text: If ’twere not she, I cannot tell who told me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: A parlous boy! Go to, you are too shrewd.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Good madam, be not angry with the child.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Pitchers have ears.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Here comes a messenger.—What news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Such news, my lord, as grieves me to report.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: How doth the Prince?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Well, madam, and in health.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What is thy news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Lord Rivers and Lord Grey are sent to Pomfret,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, with them, Sir Thomas Vaughan, prisoners.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Who hath committed them?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The mighty dukes, Gloucester and Buckingham.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: For what offense?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The sum of all I can, I have disclosed.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Why, or for what, the nobles were committed

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Is all unknown to me, my gracious lord.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ay me! I see the ruin of my house.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: The tiger now hath seized the gentle hind.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Insulting tyranny begins to jut

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Upon the innocent and aweless throne.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Welcome, destruction, blood, and massacre.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I see, as in a map, the end of all.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Accursèd and unquiet wrangling days,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: How many of you have mine eyes beheld?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: My husband lost his life to get the crown,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And often up and down my sons were tossed

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For me to joy, and weep, their gain and loss.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And being seated, and domestic broils

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Clean overblown, themselves the conquerors

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Make war upon themselves, brother to brother,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Blood to blood, self against self. O, preposterous

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And frantic outrage, end thy damnèd spleen,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Or let me die, to look on Earth no more.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Come, come, my boy. We will to sanctuary.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Madam, farewell.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Stay, I will go with you.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: You have no cause.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: My gracious lady, go,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And thither bear your treasure and your goods.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: For my part, I’ll resign unto your Grace

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The seal I keep; and so betide to me

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: As well I tender you and all of yours.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Go. I’ll conduct you to the sanctuary.
